title: B.Sc(Hons. with Research) in Zoology
content: *4-year Undergraduate Degree (Honours with Research) S. No. | Broad Category of Course | Minimum Credit Requirement for a4-Year UG Programme | 1 | Major (Core) | 80 | 2 | Minor Stream | 32 | 3 | Multidisciplinary | 09 | 4 | Ability Enhancement Courses (AEC) | 08 | 5 | Skill Enhancement Courses (SEC) | 09 | 6 | Value Added Courses common for all UG | 06-08 | 7 | Summer Internship | 02-04 | 8 | Research Project/ Dissertation | 12 | 9 | Total | 160 | 4-year UG Degree with Honours (Source: - UGC Curriculum & Credit Framework for Undergraduate Programmes) XIIth with Science (PCB) with minimum 50% marks. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list For Migration from Other Universities: 1) B.Sc (Hons/Hons with Research) Zoology Ist yr. minimum 50% marks or 5.0 CGPA on a 10 point scale 2) XII with Science with minimum 50% marks. 01 To provide an in-depth understanding of animal biology, including structure, function, development, evolution, behavior, ecology, and taxonomy, with an emphasis on fostering critical thinking and scientific inquiry. 02 To equip students with research skills and scientific methodologies necessary for pursuing advanced studies or careers in zoology, wildlife conservation, environmental management, biotechnology, and allied fields. 01 02 03 04 05 06 07 08 01 The graduated young minds will be ignited to understand the world of animal science concepts through application based learning. 02 03 The graduates of zoology will be trained for self-directed learning, recognizing, continuing educational needs in occupying positions in research, industries and related organization. 01 Comprehensive Understanding of Animal Biology: Graduates will demonstrate in-depth knowledge of animal structure, function, development, evolution, ecology, and behavior, with the ability to apply this understanding in analyzing biological systems and solving zoological problems. 02 Research Competence and Scientific Inquiry: Graduates will develop the skills necessary for independent scientific research, including hypothesis formulation, experimental design, data analysis, and scientific communication, preparing them for higher studies, research careers, and interdisciplinary collaborations in life sciences. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list For Migration from Other Universities: 1) B.Sc (Hons/Hons with Research) Zoology Ist yr. minimum 50% marks or 5.0 CGPA on a 10 point scale 2) XII with Science with minimum 50% marks.
